meta.tagsNote: Tags are descriptive area labels: for each project, skill, or job they list which fields that entry can support when assembling a resume. Games is the primary domain but tags are not games-only. Tags are generous and overlapping by design. They also drive optional `--target` filtering at build time, where untagged entries always appear and tagged entries appear when they share a tag with the target.

tagLegend.systems: Software architecture, decoupling, and systems engineering.
tagLegend.ai: Game AI, behavior, and decision systems.
tagLegend.tools: Tooling, editor workflow, content authoring, and pipelines.
tagLegend.data: Data access, databases, and data pipelines.

gameplay.label: Gameplay and Systems Programmer
gameplay.summary: Computer science student building production-style gameplay systems in Unreal Engine 5 on the Lyra framework. Strong focus on where logic lives, decoupling between systems, and data-driven authoring so content extends without new systems code. Primary work is a modular combat, encounter, and persistence architecture for an action rogue-lite.

ai.label: AI and Gameplay Engineer
ai.summary: Builds enemy and encounter logic that stays readable and designer-tunable as it grows: Behavior Tree and EQS driven AI, data-asset and wave-definition encounter pacing, and a combo and attack-routing system that resolves moves from Gameplay Tags at runtime so upgrades change behavior without branching logic.

tools.label: Tools and Core Tech Engineer
tools.summary: Builds reliable systems with a usability and content-authoring focus, so the intended way to use a system is the way a designer would expect it to work: Game Feature plugin isolation, pluggable save backends, subsystem-owned travel and persistence, and small authoring tools written during jams to speed iteration.

qa.label: Quality Assurance Engineer
qa.summary: Comes from hands-on development and structured electronics troubleshooting. Habitual at isolating issues, reproducing edge cases, and validating fixes against intended design as systems iterate. Comfortable comparing observed behavior to design intent and communicating defects clearly.

basics.name: Nicholas Reardon
basics.label: Gameplay and Systems Programmer
basics.summary: Gameplay and systems programmer building production-style Unreal Engine 5 systems on the Lyra framework. Focused on C++, modular data-driven architecture, combat, encounters, persistence, and designer-friendly content workflows.

highlights: Expected graduation: Fall 2026.

work.name: Cheapest iPhone Repair
highlights: Independently diagnosed and repaired unfamiliar devices through structured troubleshooting, fault isolation, and verification testing.
Executed delicate device teardowns with detailed tracking and documentation for reliable reassembly.
Built internal repair documentation and improved workflows to increase consistency and efficiency.

projects.name: Tethered
projects.description: Lead system designer and gameplay engineer for a Lyra-based Unreal Engine action rogue-lite focused on modular combat, encounters, seamless travel, persistence, and data-driven content authoring.
highlights: Architected a modular Lyra-based gameplay framework using Game Feature plugin isolation to keep combat, game modes, and content decoupled while iterating on an arena-mode vertical slice.
Designed a world-level event bus on the GameState Ability System Component to coordinate encounter, spawn, and travel systems at runtime without coupling individual components.
Built a seamless-travel subsystem that snapshots and reconstructs player and world state across level transitions, preserving GAS attributes, equipment, and run upgrades through GameState teardown.
Implemented a pluggable save backend behind an interface, isolating disk I/O from runtime state and leaving a clean extension point for a future server-backed implementation.
Designed a designer-facing combo system using an input-tag routing map where each step resolves attacks from owned Gameplay Tags at runtime, letting upgrades modify movesets without branching logic.
Built data-driven encounter, wave-advance, spawn-modifier, and combo-routing systems so new encounter rules, enemy behaviors, and upgrades can be authored through data.
Migrated a GAS-based Lyra project across two major Unreal Engine versions (5.6 to 5.8), resolving API deprecations, build and registration breakage, and Lyra base-class changes.

projects.name: Alien Survivors
projects.description: Solo Unity prototype with data-driven weapons, upgrades, enemy spawning, and runtime UI.
highlights: Built an enemy spawning director with spawn pacing, cap management, and optional object pooling for performance under high enemy counts.
Implemented rarity-weighted enemy selection with per-type parameters and runtime tuning hooks for spawn rate and movement speed.
Built a fully data-driven weapon and upgrade framework with stat scaling and functional modifiers authored as editor-defined upgrade definitions.
Created dynamic runtime UI that adapts to available upgrades and player state as new weapons and modifiers are added.

projects.name: Last Oasis
projects.description: Primary gameplay and tooling programmer for a browser-playable Godot game jam platformer.
highlights: Built a nested state-machine character controller with ground, air, wall-slide, slam, destroy, and death states; coyote time; variable-height jumping; wall jumps; input buffering; spike grace frames; and tile-data-driven slippery surfaces.
Built a chain-reaction destructible system where a facing raycast breaks a target and an expanding overlap query breaks neighbors outward with per-depth delay.
Created an editor-time tile-to-scene tool that swaps tiles for scene instances keyed by tile custom data while preserving flip, transpose, and rotation values.
Built an aimable launch ability with aim, fire, and cancel states, a live guide line, and on-screen control prompts.

projects.name: A Totally Normal Bike Ride
projects.description: Primary programmer for a three-day Godot jam game about physics balancing a bear on a unicycle across telephone wires.
highlights: Built a two-body physics character using a wheel and rider rigidbody joined by a pin joint, with torque-based locomotion and separate ground and air balance tuning.
Implemented a charge-and-release variable jump with compression, impulse scaling, contact-based ground state, and rolling average-speed feedback.
Built a telephone-wire authoring tool that creates segmented physics ropes between anchors and renders them as smooth baked Curve2D lines.
Implemented a shader-driven day-night cycle with four-phase sky blending, scripted sun and moon motion, stars, and tinted clouds.

projects.name: Stock Backtesting
projects.description: Python and PySide6 desktop application for downloading market data, charting ETF price history, and running trading-strategy backtests. Educational engineering project, not production trading software.
highlights: Built a PySide6 desktop interface for selecting ETFs, date ranges, and strategies, with embedded Matplotlib charts for price history and strategy performance.
Designed a data-access abstraction with swappable adapters for Yahoo Finance, local JSON, Alpha Vantage, and Polygon-style sources, so adding a provider does not touch consumer code.
Added dynamic strategy loading via Python module discovery so a new strategy drops in as a file with no controller changes, and ran data downloads on a background QThread to keep the GUI responsive.
Implemented SMA Crossover, MACD, and Bollinger Bands strategies on shared infrastructure for tracking cash and shares, logging trades, computing return metrics, and writing CSV outputs.
